name: Resolution Financial Services
description_short: Resolution Accounting provides outsourced bookkeeping, accounting, and part-time CFO advisory services to small businesses in the Exton, PA area.
description_long:
Resolution Accounting is a professional accounting firm based in Exton, Pennsylvania, offering comprehensive financial management services to small business owners. The company positions itself as a one-stop solution for businesses struggling to manage their financial operations while focusing on core business activities.

The firm provides three core service offerings: outsourced bookkeeping (including oversight of in-house bookkeepers), accounting services for accurate financial reporting, and part-time CFO advisory services. They specialize in helping early-stage and growing companies that cannot afford full-time CFO salaries but need professional financial oversight. Their services include bookkeeping cleanup and catch-up, financial statement preparation, cash flow forecasting, strategic financial planning, and tax preparation coordination with external tax accountants.

Resolution Accounting distinguishes itself through integrated service delivery—all three levels of financial services available under one roof, eliminating the need for business owners to coordinate with multiple vendors. They emphasize personalized, one-on-one support and claim to tailor software solutions and accounting packages to client needs. The company communicates directly with clients' tax accountants during tax season and positions themselves as continuously available advisors rather than transactional service providers.

The company is best suited for small business owners who need professional financial management but lack internal accounting expertise or bandwidth. However, potential clients should note that the website lacks specific pricing information, service-level agreements, credentials of individual team members, and concrete examples of client results. There is no indication this is a free service despite the category—this appears to be a paid professional services firm.
pros: Integrated service model allows businesses to access bookkeeping, accounting, and CFO services from a single provider without coordination overhead
Specialized focus on small business sector with stated depth of experience in early-stage company financial challenges
Offers part-time CFO advisory at claimed cost below a single mid-range employee salary, addressing affordability for startups
Provides cleanup and catch-up services for disorganized or behind bookkeeping, not just ongoing maintenance
Direct communication with client tax accountants during tax season to streamline tax preparation process
Emphasizes accessibility with one-on-one support and rapid response ('just a phone call or quick message away')
Offers software optimization and selection guidance tailored to client business processes
cons: Website contains no pricing information, making it impossible to compare cost before initial consultation
No staff credentials, certifications, or individual team member qualifications are disclosed
No client testimonials, case studies, or concrete examples of results and business impact
Geographic service area is unclear—location is Exton, PA but no statement of whether they serve remote clients
Services appear to be paid professional services despite category placement as 'free-help'; misleading categorization
best_for: Small business owners (1-50 employees) whose accounting is disorganized or falling behind and need professional cleanup
Early-stage startups and growth-phase companies that need CFO-level financial guidance but cannot afford a full-time CFO
Business owners who want to delegate financial management entirely so they can focus on sales, operations, and customer service
Companies seeking integrated bookkeeping and accounting from a single trusted provider to reduce vendor management complexity
diagnosis: Resolution Accounting is best for small business owners who need professional financial management and have disorganized books, but cannot afford a full-time accountant or CFO. The main caveat is that this is a paid professional services firm with no transparent pricing, making it impossible to assess affordability without direct contact—and it should not be categorized as 'free-help.'
services: Small business accounting and financial statement preparation
Financial forecasting and cash flow planning
Strategic financial planning for business growth
Tax preparation package coordination with external tax accountants
Software selection and accounting package optimization
Business financial analysis and margin management
